food_subgroup: Fast food combo meal — fried chicken, chicken nuggets, side dish, soft drink

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a branded fast-food combo meal. FOODON_00002490 (fried chicken food product) is the closest parent covering the primary protein component (Chickenjoy fried chicken). The combo nature including nuggets, side, and soft drink is not captured by any single FoodOn term.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (Chicken meat, cooked) is the closest defensible parent for the dominant protein component. No FoodEx2 code exists for a branded multi-component fast-food combo meal.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for this branded fast-food combo meal product.

main_ingredients: side dish (coleslaw or rice or fries — variable)

serving.description: 1 combo meal (2 pc Chickenjoy + 4 pc Chicken Nuggets + 1 side + 1 medium soft drink)
serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is a full Jollibee Fun for One Meal C (D) combo consisting of 2 pieces of Chickenjoy fried chicken (approximately 280 g), 4 pieces of chicken nuggets (approximately 80 g), one side dish such as coleslaw or rice (approximately 150 g), and one medium carbonated soft drink (approximately 240 ml / 240 g), totalling approximately 750 g.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Fried Chicken (Breaded, Bone-in, Fast Food) and Chicken Nuggets (Fast Food); composite estimation for combo meal
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for the Jollibee Fun for One Meal C (D) combo as a whole. Nutrient values per 100 g are estimated by component-weighted composition: (a) USDA FDC #172960 Fast foods, chicken, breaded and fried, boneless pieces (nuggets/tenders) and (b) USDA FDC #172959 Fast foods, chicken, breaded and fried, bone-in pieces, combined with a representative fast-food coleslaw and a medium cola soft drink, weighted by estimated gram contribution to the 750 g total serving. The soft drink contributes primarily sugar and energy; the fried chicken components dominate protein, fat, and sodium. Values represent a plausible central estimate for this combo meal type. Confidence is medium due to variability in side dish choice and exact Jollibee formulation not being publicly disclosed in an approved database.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; and University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for fried chicken (battered) and cola soft drink
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Jollibee Fun for One Meal C (D) combo. GI estimated as a weighted composite: battered fried chicken GI ~45 (low-medium, University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for breaded fried chicken); cola soft drink GI ~63 (medium, University of Sydney GI Database); coleslaw GI ~45; white rice side GI ~72 (if rice chosen). Weighted average across the combo's carbohydrate-contributing components yields an estimated GI of approximately 55 (low). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 138.75 g carbohydrate in 750 g serving) / 100 ≈ 25.7. This is an analogous estimate; direct measurement is recommended.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~380 mg per 100 g; approximately 2,850 mg per full combo serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this combo meal, as a single serving provides approximately 2,850 mg sodium — exceeding the WHO recommended daily limit of 2,000 mg — primarily from the seasoned fried chicken batter, nugget coating, and any dipping sauces.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~25.7 per serving) driven by battered coating, soft drink sugars, and starchy side dish
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this combo meal or substitute the soft drink with water and choose a lower-GI side, as the combination of refined-carbohydrate batter, sugary carbonated drink, and starchy side produces a high glycemic load (~25.7) that is likely to cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat, trans fat, sodium, and cholesterol from deep-fried battered chicken
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this combo meal, as the deep-fried battered chicken components contribute saturated fat (~2.4 g/100 g), trace trans fat (~0.1 g/100 g), and high sodium (~380 mg/100 g), all of which are associated with increased LDL cholesterol, arterial inflammation, and elevated blood pressure risk when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Jollibee Fun for One Meal C (D) is a branded fast-food combo meal sold at Jollibee restaurants in Singapore, including the Bugis Village outlet. It consists of 2 pieces of Chickenjoy fried chicken (available in Original or Spicy variants, or a mix), 4 pieces of Chicken Nuggets (Original or Spicy), one choice of side dish (such as coleslaw, rice, or fries), and one medium carbonated soft drink. Chickenjoy is Jollibee's signature bone-in, skin-on fried chicken, marinated in a proprietary blend of spices and coated in a crispy seasoned batter before deep-frying, yielding a juicy interior with a crunchy, flavourful exterior. The chicken nuggets are processed chicken pieces coated in a light batter and deep-fried. The meal is certified Halal in Singapore. Nutritionally, this combo is energy-dense (~198 kcal/100 g; approximately 1,485 kcal per full 750 g serving), high in protein (~11.2 g/100 g from chicken), moderate-to-high in fat (~9.8 g/100 g, predominantly from deep-frying oil), and high in refined carbohydrates (~18.5 g/100 g) from the batter coating and soft drink. Sodium content is high (~380 mg/100 g; ~2,850 mg per serving), making this meal a concern for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic load per serving is estimated at approximately 25.7 (medium-high), driven by the battered coating, sugary soft drink, and starchy side. This meal is best consumed occasionally and is not suitable for daily consumption by individuals with cardiometabolic conditions.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for the Jollibee Fun for One Meal C (D) combo as a complete dish. Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted composition using USDA FoodData Central entries for fast-food fried chicken (bone-in, breaded) and chicken nuggets, combined with representative values for coleslaw and cola soft drink. The exact Jollibee proprietary formulation, marinade, and batter recipe are not publicly disclosed in any approved database. GI is an analogous estimate. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are per 100 g of the full combo meal (all components combined, weighted by gram contribution to the ~750 g total serving). 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source entries used for this estimation. 3. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source entries used for this estimation. 4. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source entries used for this estimation. 5. Trans fat is present at a trace level (~0.1 g/100 g) from deep-frying; this is a plausible estimate based on USDA data for similar fast-food fried chicken products. 6. The side dish choice (coleslaw, rice, or fries) introduces variability; values reflect a weighted average assuming a mix of likely side choices. 7. GI is an analogous estimate (approximately 55, low) based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for breaded fried chicken and cola soft drink; direct measurement is recommended. 8. This meal is Halal-certified at Jollibee Singapore outlets. 9. The meal is not suitable for individuals on sodium-restricted, low-GI, or low-saturated-fat diets without significant modification.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Drafted full record with component-weighted nutrient estimation from USDA FoodData Central. Established multilingual names for all five mandatory languages (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) in notes. Set GI to 55 (medium) as analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database. Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list.

Iteration 2: Addressed validator feedback for Q4. Corrected `gi_category` from 'medium' to 'low' for `glycemic_index` 55, as per University of Sydney GI Database classification (low GI is <= 55). Updated `gi_source.source_match_notes` to reflect this change. All hard gates pass, score 8/8.
